# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 137

This is turn 137. Turns 1 through 136 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 137.

## Turn 137 — the quartic obstruction compresses to one bit

The \(h_0\)-trial word remains unchanged. It specializes to the corrected marked pro-\(2\) relation
\[
r_{\mathrm{new}}=x_0^{\sigma^2}x_0[x_1,\sigma],
\]
whereas the retired word specializes to
\[
r_{\mathrm{old}}=x_0^2x_1^3x_1^\sigma.
\]
Turn 136 proved that their first framed difference is the quartic functional
\[
\kappa=[ABAB],
\]
which is nonzero on every cubic-matching old-to-new residual.  

### 1. The entire \(15\)-dimensional fourth layer compresses to \(C_2\)

Let \(F\) be free pro-\(2\) on \(\sigma,x_0,x_1\), and put
\[
J_4=F/\langle D_5F,r_{\mathrm{new}}\rangle,\qquad
J_3=J_4/D_4J_4.
\]

Write
\[
Z_4=D_4J_4.
\]
The restricted-Lie dimensions are
\[
\dim L_4=24,\qquad \dim I_4=9,
\]
so
\[
Z_4\simeq \mathbf F_2^{15},\qquad |J_3|=2^{13},\qquad |J_4|=2^{28}.
\]

Direct binary linear algebra gives
\[
\kappa(I_4)=0,\qquad \kappa(\Delta_4)=1.
\]
Thus \(\kappa\) descends to a nonzero functional
\[
\kappa:Z_4\longrightarrow \mathbf F_2.
\]

Its full exact-\(C_4\)-framed stabilizer orbit is a singleton:

- every framed IA automorphism acts trivially on the associated graded fourth layer;
- the only nonidentity linear stabilizer is
  \[
  A\mapsto A+C,\qquad B\mapsto B,\qquad C\mapsto C,
  \]
  and it fixes the coefficient of \(ABAB\).

Therefore
\[
\operatorname{span}\bigl(\operatorname{Aut}_{C_4}(J_3)\cdot\kappa\bigr)
=\langle\kappa\rangle.
\]

Define
\[
\boxed{\;
Q_4:=J_4/\ker\kappa .
\;}
\]
Then
\[
|Q_4|=|J_3|\cdot2=2^{14}.
\]

This is minimal among exact-\(C_4\)-framed characteristic central quotients retaining the full common third jet: a separating quotient must retain at least one fourth-layer bit, and \(\kappa\) alone suffices.

### 2. Exact framed pro-\(2\) counts

The central extension
\[
1\longrightarrow Z_4\longrightarrow J_4\longrightarrow J_3\longrightarrow1
\]
has \(Z_4\subseteq\Phi(J_4)\). Hence its transgression
\[
Z_4^\vee\hookrightarrow H^2(J_3,\mathbf F_2)
\]
is injective. Distinct nonzero fourth-layer functionals therefore give distinct central pushouts over \(J_3\).

Every exact-\(C_4\)-framed automorphism of \(J_3\) lifts to \(Q_4\). Indeed, composing any such automorphism with a fixed old-to-new cubic identification produces another old-to-new identification; Turn 136 says both have \(\kappa\)-residual \(1\), so their difference has residual \(0\).

Consequently every exact-framed epimorphism from the corrected marked Demuškin group onto \(Q_4\) has the same kernel \(\ker\kappa\). Postcomposition therefore acts simply transitively:
\[
\boxed{\;
|\operatorname{Sur}_{\nu}(D_{\mathrm{new}},Q_4)|
=
|\operatorname{Aut}_{\nu}(Q_4)|.
\;}
\]
Thus its normalized framed count is exactly \(1\).

For the retired relation,
\[
\boxed{\;
\operatorname{Sur}_{\nu}(D_{\mathrm{old}},Q_4)=\varnothing,
\;}
\]
because every cubic-compatible map has \(\kappa\)-residual \(1\).

So the \(2^{28}\)-element fourth jet has been replaced by a \(2^{14}\)-element target with the same separating power.

### 3. Uniform tame pullbacks

For \(m\ge2\), define
\[
Q_m=Q_4\times_{C_4}C_{2^m}.
\]
Let \(e\) be odd with
\[
\operatorname{ord}_e(2)=2^m,
\]
and put
\[
T_{e,m}=C_e\rtimes C_{2^m},
\qquad t^s=t^2,
\]
\[
G_{e,m}=T_{e,m}\times_{C_{2^m}}Q_m.
\]

Equivalently,
\[
G_{e,m}=C_e\rtimes Q_m,
\]
where \(q\in Q_m\) acts on \(C_e\) by multiplication by \(2^{\nu(q)}\).

Its \(2\)-core is
\[
O_2(G_{e,m})=\ker(\nu:Q_m\to C_{2^m}).
\]
Indeed, every normal \(2\)-subgroup must centralize \(C_e\); faithfulness of the action forces its unramified coordinate to vanish.

### 4. Complete surjection count for the repaired word

Take a surjection from the \(h_0\)-candidate to \(G_{e,m}\). Since \(x_0,x_1\in O_2(G_{e,m})\), write
\[
x_i=(0,q_i),\qquad q_i\in\ker\nu.
\]
The projection of \(\tau\) to the \(2\)-group \(Q_m\) is trivial, so
\[
\tau=(b,1).
\]
Surjectivity forces \(b\) to generate \(C_e\), giving \(\varphi(e)\) choices.

Write
\[
\sigma=(a,q_\sigma).
\]
The tame relation and the fact that \(b\) generates \(C_e\) force
\[
\nu(q_\sigma)=1\pmod{2^m}.
\]
The component \(a\in C_e\) is arbitrary, giving \(e\) choices.

Because every \(q_i\in\ker\nu\) centralizes \(C_e\),
\[
u_i=x_i,\qquad d_0=c_0=1,
\]
and the \(h_0\)-relation reduces exactly to
\[
q_0^{q_\sigma^2}q_0[q_1,q_\sigma]=1.
\]
Hence the \(Q_m\)-component is precisely an exact-framed epimorphism
\[
D_{\mathrm{new}}\twoheadrightarrow Q_m.
\]

Therefore
\[
\boxed{\;
|\operatorname{Sur}(\Gamma_{h_0},G_{e,m})|
=
e\,\varphi(e)\,
|\operatorname{Aut}_{\nu}(Q_m)|.
\;}
\tag{137.1}
\]

### 5. Automorphisms give the same number

The subgroup \(C_e\) is characteristic in \(G_{e,m}\). An automorphism consists of:

- a unit automorphism of \(C_e\): \(\varphi(e)\) choices;
- an automorphism of \(Q_m\) preserving \(\nu\);
- a \(1\)-cocycle \(Q_m\to C_e\).

Coprime cohomology gives
\[
H^1(Q_m,C_e)=0.
\]
Moreover \(C_e^{Q_m}=1\), since an invariant \(c\) satisfies \(2c=c\). Thus
\[
|Z^1(Q_m,C_e)|=|C_e|=e.
\]
Consequently
\[
\boxed{\;
|\operatorname{Aut}(G_{e,m})|
=
e\,\varphi(e)\,
|\operatorname{Aut}_{\nu}(Q_m)|.
\;}
\tag{137.2}
\]

Combining (137.1) and (137.2),
\[
\boxed{\;
\frac{|\operatorname{Sur}(\Gamma_{h_0},G_{e,m})|}
     {|\operatorname{Aut}(G_{e,m})|}
=1.
\;}
\]

The corrected marked local maximal pro-\(2\) group has the same characteristic quotient \(Q_m\), while \(T_{e,m}\) is the canonical tame quotient. Their fiber product therefore gives one local automorphism orbit as well:
\[
\boxed{\;
N_{\mathbf Q_2}(G_{e,m})=1.
\;}
\]

For the retired \(A_2\)-word, projection to \(Q_4\) would give a forbidden old-to-new framed epimorphism, so
\[
\boxed{\;
\frac{|\operatorname{Sur}(\Gamma_{A_2},G_{e,m})|}
     {|\operatorname{Aut}(G_{e,m})|}
=0.
\;}
\]

Thus the complete uniform count is
\[
\begin{array}{c|ccc}
 & G_{\mathbf Q_2} & h_0\text{-trial} & \text{retired }A_2\\ \hline
G_{e,m} & 1 & 1 & 0.
\end{array}
\]

This is exactly the verifier normalization \( |\mathrm{Sur}|/|\mathrm{Aut}| \) specified in the problem. 

The smallest member has \(e=5,m=2\):
\[
|Q_4|=2^{14},\qquad |G_{5,2}|=5\cdot2^{14}=81920.
\]
The \(e=17,m=3\) member uses \(|Q_3|=2^{15}\), so the previously proposed two tame checks are now subsumed by one count-one family.

**ESTABLISHED**: The stabilizer orbit of \(\kappa=[ABAB]\) is one-dimensional, so the \(15\)-dimensional fourth layer compresses to the minimal characteristic quotient \(Q_4\) of order \(2^{14}\). Proved exact framed counts: the corrected marked group has one automorphism orbit onto \(Q_4\), while the retired relation has none. For every \(\operatorname{ord}_e(2)=2^m\), proved
\[
|\operatorname{Sur}(\Gamma_{h_0},G_{e,m})|=|\operatorname{Aut}(G_{e,m})|,
\]
so the repaired candidate and the true local group both have normalized count \(1\), while the retired word has count \(0\).

**GAP**: This closes the full fourth-order diagnostic family, but it does not test degree-five tame–wild gluing. The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun, and the \(h_0\)-word is still a trial presentation.

**NEXT**: Replace isolated fifth-order targets by a characteristic obstruction tower: compute the exact-\(C_4\)-framed action on \(D_5/D_6\), identify the smallest invariant quotient carrying the difference between the \(h_0\)-word and the true marked Demuškin lift, and derive its tame-pullback count before examining degree six.
